arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's response to Middlesbrough coaching team 'experience' theory
arts.sum: Michael Carrick dismissed the theory that he could be helped by having a more experienced coach on his backroom team, insisting he is happy with the dynamics of his current Middlesbrough team. While Graeme has around 15 years worth of coaching experience at the FA and Newcastle United, while Carrick and Woodgate have various experiences in their young coaching careers too, it’s often heard from some supporters that an older head with greater experience could really help the Boro head coach.

arts.ttl: 'It would be easy' - What's pleased Michael Carrick most about Middlesbrough's January signings - Teesside Live
arts.sum: Michael Carrick has praised Middlesbrough’s January signings for quickly settling and assuming responsibility to help turn the side’s current fortunes - admitting it would be easy for them to down tools or be disheartened. As Carrick and his players continued working hard at Rockliffe in the past week as they prepare for the trip to Bristol City, the Boro head coach admitted the character and professionalism shown from the new faces - four of whom are only loan players too - has been great.
